title: Hexgears Impulse - Hot-swap Mechanical Keyboard (Box Brown Switches, Pudding Keycaps)
description:
The Hexgears Impulse is available in many amazing configurations.
— Durable PBT Keycaps
PBT is an engineering plastic that resists shine and finger grease. This is far better than the cheap plastic used by major brands. All Impulses use PBT in their doubleshot injection molded keycaps — the main letters won’t ever wear off.
— Awesome RGB Backlighting and RGB Ring Lighting
The Impulse lighting system is on-board, ensuring compatibility with various operating systems. Translucent pudding keycaps showcase its smooth RGB lighting in a way that other keyboards can only dream of. RGB customization options allow you to select an experience that matches your battlestation.
— Survives Your Worst Impulses
Do you have a coffee habit? A cat that spills things on the regular? The Impulse handles liquids like a champ. IP56 water resistance means that the keyboard functions while blasted with water jets. Its curved metal frame is also quite rigid.
— Kailh Box Switches
Kailh Box switches are rated for 80 million presses — way above industry standard. They have drainage ports and self-cleaning housings. Box switches also feel excellent, as they’re less wobbly and noticeably smoother than standard models. Tactile Box switches result in relatively quiet mechanical keyboards, but the clicky versions go all out with a new and improved mechanism.
— Other Features
The Impulse is designed as a plug and play device. Its feature set offers exactly what you need to get up and running fast. 2-stage angle adjustment feet and basic onboard functions like Windows key locking enhance your experience.
Impulse keyboards have a 1-year limited warranty.
features: RGB, Per-key customizable lighting modes, RGB Light Ring
Two-shot extra durable PBT keycaps (Pudding & 2-tone)
Curved metal mounting plate with 2 stage adjustable height feet
IP56 water resistant design, N-key Rollover (NKRO), integrated USB-A cable
Available in Kailh BOX Brown (Tactile), BOX White (Clicky), and Hako Clear (Heavy Tactile) Mechanical Switches
Updated Impulse V2 design with Hot-swap switch sockets (MX-compatible, LED compatible, plate mount)

reviews.title: Good Keyboard, clicky, and positive return
reviews.text:
Hi, I am a bit of a keyboard junky. I have bought probably 10 mechanical keyboards over the last 3 years. I still have an original IBM PC keyboard - that is really stiff and loud. But about this keyboard, I read an online review and said, why not - and I have been using it for several days. First, it sets up easy. Just plug in to get started. Configuring all the lights is more work and not completely intuitive - especially if you lose the little manual 3x4" or so. Personally, I don't care that much for the lighting features on any keyboard - I want to know if I can type quickly without any errors from the keyboard as opposed from me. In general, this keyboard is one of the best. The key depression effort is light to medium - if you rest your fingers on the keys, they will not trigger -which is good. The space bar works well - and I usually find this a problem because I hit it a bit off center - but this one works well. The noise is medium. The clacky sound is the sound of me pressing the keys to the maximum and not the click of the membrane.

I generally like this keyboard and I have tried many from $79 mechanical keys to $200 keyboards. I think this is up there with the best - maybe not all the bells and whistles like volume control, but I never use that. Buy this. Works well. Feels good under the fingers. Type fast!

reviews.title: Recommended for Gaming
reviews.text: Nice keyboard. Has NKRO, macro support, and lots of lighting patterns for the keyboard and ring lighting. Looks very nice in the Pudding keycaps version with full RGB. I ordered it with the Kailh Box Brown switches, which work smoothly, and have a somewhat subtle tactile feedback. I tested it fully, and there was no key chatter detected. Well worth the reasonable asking price and one of the very few current options offering these switches in a commercial build.

reviews.title: Great for typing
reviews.text:
I have the version with white keycaps and "box white" clicky switches. I don't play computer games, so I can't comment on that aspect of the keyboard. But I can say that this is a very good typist's keyboard. The key switches sound like Cherry Blues, but I think these feel smoother, and have less lateral wiggle room.

The keyboard comes with a tiny "user guide" that is no more than a list of keystrokes that control the lighting and macros. I don't care for lighting on a keyboard, so the first thing I tried to do was dim the lights. The "user guide" wasn't very clear on that, so here's how:

While holding down the "Fn" key...
... press the Down arrow to dim the white lights.
... press the "2" key on the numeric keypad to dim the colored perimeter lights.

Keep pressing those keys to dim the lights further. I've now got them completely dimmed, and I hope they stay that way.

One thing to know about this keyboard is that the keycaps aren't recessed. On most keyboards, you can't see or grasp the bottoms of the keycaps, because they fit into cavities formed by the molded plastic deck. This keyboard's top deck is flat metal, without cavities. So there's about a quarter-inch gap between the keycap bottoms and the top of the deck. As a result, it's easy to pull the keycaps. You can even remove the Escape key with your bare fingers. I don't think this is a bad thing; it's just worth knowing about.

Speaking of keycaps, these use a stylized font that sacrifices legibility for fashion. The designer apparently dislikes closed shapes, and created little vertical gaps in the Q, O, zero, 8, etc. Not even the ampersand, pound sign, or the little orbs of the percent sign are spared this annoying treatment. It looks like the dye is flaking away. As a sometimes hunt-and-peck typist, I find this very distracting, as it keeps me from instantly recognizing many of the keys.

I would rate this keyboard with five stars, if not for the inadequate "user guide" and the poorly thought-out keycap font.

reviews.title: Very well built
reviews.text: Solid keys, good feel.  I don't need all of the LEDS, and had to play around with the controls to get just the minimum lighting.  Much better than my old "mushy" wireless keyboard.

title: YMDK ANSI ISO PBT Doubleshot Shine Through Pudding Keycaps Suitable for MX Mechanical Keyboard Corsair Razer SF KBD75 Tada68 DZ60 (Black Pudding Full Set)(Only Keycap)

reviews.title: Great but some keys may not be suitable for your keyboard
reviews.text: they look really pretty tbh. However some keys had fitment issues. The spacebar had their slots just a little bit off so it couldnt fit. The caps lock is upside down. The windows and menu keys did not like being next to other keys. Other than that, the rest of the keys fit great.

reviews.title: They work great
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:eaa9e5229cc02ed31da7ef98d0a78ee4]] Replaced the most of my bottom row on my keyboard with these key caps.  They are good, the LED shines through and it really nice! for Corsair keyboards.

reviews.title: Quality can be improved but overall great price and compatibility
reviews.text: One of the few sets that actually fit my K95 (Corsair bottom row) Plenty of extra pieces for other keyboards too. The color black of these pieces appear much more grey compared to he caps my keyboard came with. And each piece has a little dimple at the top where they were cast or molded. Not the end of the world, but other sets don't have that. Also found small imperfections in several pieces. Time will tell how they do over prolonged use. Pretty good for the price, solid 4 of 5.

reviews.title: Just what I needed
reviews.text: I wanted some keycaps to match the theme of my keyboard but it's a struggle finding keycaps that fit the sizing of R1 on a Corsair keyboard. This is exactly what I needed!

title: Akko Keycaps Top Printed Shine-Through with ASA Profile, White Keycaps PBT Double-Shot Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards
features: SHINE-THROUGH KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Shine-through keycap sets! Come with extremely durable and resistant double-shot Shine-through keycaps, the legend shine through keycaps brings unique and great RGB effects.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Not the brightest shine-through, but for the material and price it's worth it.
reviews.text:
Akko's ASA profile is a taller profile kind of like SA. Feels great to type on and is fine for gaming if you don't mind the extra travel time on each keystroke. The finish on these has a great texture and it's PBT so it shouldn't wear out quickly. "Shouldn't" being relative since I tend to shred keycaps as though my fingers were first dipped in acid before typing.

30.00 for keycaps of this quality and for the number of keys is great especially in a hobby where everything is over priced, over hyped, and overly bogged down in group buys and pre-orders that take literal years to fulfill.

reviews.title: Shine-Through keycaps, look amazing
reviews.text: These shine-through keycaps look awesome.  They are super easy to install, like most keycaps.  It is just a bit time consuming.  I love being able to change out keys and switches and seem to collect keycaps.  These are great and sound and feel amazing.

This gaming keyboard is equipped with floating ABS Keycaps that are removable and come with 8 extra silicon keycaps. This Gaming keyboard is also FULLY WATER RESISTANT and will not break now in when accidents happen – 100% anti-ghosting & full N-key rollover over USB and fully programmable with advanced macros.

【Console Compatibility】Compatible with Xbox One to play keyboard enabled games

reviews.title: Where's the documentation?
reviews.text: Very heavy keyboard. Certainly doesn't feel like a mechanical keyboard. The tiny insert offers ZERO CLUES as to how the keyboard is supposed to function, ie there is no documentation. The web site reads more like an ad, no clues there, either. The flashing color LEDs remind me of a 1980s video arcade game. You can reduce the brightness down to zero but that's about it. There's a really ugly and difficult to read quasi-sci-fi typeface used on the key caps. The keyboard itself is quite nice, quite heavy. Little kickstand-type feet if you want it angled. A small plastic ramp is included if  you want to rest  your left hand. What's missing here is a MANUAL of some kind. Even a PDF would be just great. Otherwise it's a mystery how this keyboard is supposed to work, what the features are. Somebody blew it. It's nicely designed with some interesting features, but unless you enjoy hardware sleuthing, how are you supposed to know what works, and how it works? Rather maddening. So I don't see this being used, I'll go back to my older keyboard which actually has mechanical clickity click key responses. It's baffling that with  the attention to detail paid to this keyboard that they wouldn't include or provide some DOCUMENTATION. It's basic.

reviews.title: This is an amazing keyboard! With this and the mouse, I am ecstatic! Don't hesitate on this! Can I give it 6 stars?
reviews.text:
Oh wow! I have waited for several days to review this because my computer was broken and I had to wait for a service tech to drive down and DO something, ANYTHING, with it. When the box came, I was asking myself what it was because the box was HUGE, The inner box was still pretty big, but not huge, It provides a lot of information--including that the keyboard is waterproof, nice since I'm a klutz--and something I'd either not noticed or had forgotten. In side that was a plain black box with a Bloody hand--or is it a hand print? The lights are cool, and the bloody hand lights up on both the keyboard and the Bloody Mouse I got a couple of days before the keyboard arrived. The fast key response is amazing. Even though I'm not a "gamer," I'm not a total computer novice, having gotten my first one with a gig on the hard drive--custom built--and my co-workers said I'd never use all that memory. Right. Now, my phone has 64 times that with room for expansion and does more than that first computer--or the second--and maybe more than the fifth or sixth. I still have to download the software, which will probably wait until tomorrow, but I could not wait to start playing with this. It will take me a little bit to get used to the clicking keys, but I am finding I don't mind it, after just writing this much and, well, it does bring back memories. Also, they help me notice if I don't hit a key quite hard enough. (My high school graduation present was a used Royal portable typewriter in a horrible shade of blue--and I still dislike blue. But, I think I can get past those memories. My parents weren't cheap. They were broke at that point in time.) And now, I have probably spent more on this keyboard, even though I was amazingly fortunate to be able to get this at a discount in exchange for my honest, unbiased review, than my family spent on that typewriter.

I didn't know what to expect of this, but read what one of the other reviewers said about not needing to be a gamer to appreciate this and I can see, already, how true it is. My typing is faster and I'm just using this for the first time. And now with this and the matching mouse, I can dare to try some of the things I hear about. No, even with this pair, I won't have the fast response time of someone a few decades (well, quite a few) younger, but it will sure help even the playing field for me. I am so eager to put this through its paces and I am SO happy with it, even this soon, that I have no problem recommending anything with the Bloody name on it. And I did live in New Zealand for a while, so know that Bloody is not quite considered a polite word. Too Bloody bad. I love the keyboard and mouse!

Addendum: Two days along and I'm even happier! Wow! This thing is great!

reviews.title: Keyed Up
reviews.text: I don't do a lot of gaming but this is nicer than the old Dell keyboard I was using.  It's kind of loud but otherwise I haven't had any problems typing.  The keys light up when the computer is on and during idle times it puts on a little light show.  I use the old home row technique and haven't seen any increase in errors.  Hunt and peck types might feel differently. I already have a wrist rest so I haven't used the one that attaches to the keyboard.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard even if it's not mechanical
reviews.text:
Nice keys that feel similar to mechanical. Spacebar is set up so you can press it on the edges and it will respond just as well as hitting the middle. Larger keys such as Backspace, Return, Ctrl, and the Windows all work fine.

QWER and ASDF keys are colored orange and have a unique, bumpy texture you don't feel on the smooth black keys. I would have preferred orange keys on ASDF and JKL; since that is "Home Row" for typists. The bumpy texture would make it easier to find Home Row without looking.

Arrow keys are the common one on top and three below configuration. The keys I don't often see are a FN key and a weird key with no text label instead of a second Windows key on the right of the spacebar. The "weird" key seems to work as if you right-clicked the mouse. Finally, you will see the Print Screen (PS), Scroll Lock (SL), and Page/Break (PB) keys placed above the numeric keypad.

Aside from the extra keys, the slightly increased resistance, lit keys, and the increased weight of the keyboard itself, there isn't much else that separates this from a standard keyboard. All keys stay lit when you type and switch to rotating patterns after a minute or two of inactivity. Hit any key and you are back to fully lit.

reviews.title: Solid and positive
reviews.text:
There is a lot to like about the Bloody Gaming LED B720 Light Strike Mechanical Gaming Keyboard. Fit and feel, construction, lighting scheme, and most importantly “mechanical keys” are all first rate.

Right out of the box, you can feel the weight of this keyboard. No lightweight here. Plugging it in, there are no issues with Windows 7 to 10 – drivers are automatically detected and the keyboard is immediately available. The keys light up with a rainbow of color, with each row presenting a different color. On this B720 model, there is no way to change the color scheme, but most people will find it pleasant. In idle mode, it presents a couple of animations, upon which any key touch will immediately bring back full lighting. Lighting is dimmable to totally “off”.

Of course, you do not purchase a keyboard like this without awareness that a variety of mechanical key types exist. My other keyboard is a Tt esport MEKA Cherry Brown with Cherry MX Brown switches. Cherry MX brown switches have a moderate travel distance, with some tactile feedback for keystroke and a definite bottom and light clicking noise. For me, Brown switches provide a nice blend for gaming and touch typing. The Bloody B720 has an Infrared Optic Switch exclusively developed by Bloody, and indicated to be closest to a Cherry MX Black switch. For me, comparing the Optic Switch to Cherry Brown switches, the throw distance seems shorter for the Bloody keys, with less resistance and the bottom just slightly softer. Click wise, it is mechanically positive, but noise wise less than the brown switch. Personally, I find both very desirable for the way I type and game, and struggle with finding a preference between “Optic black” and Cherry Brown.

On other aspects of the keyboard, the Bloody B720 comes with textured and differently colored gaming silicone keys, along with a nice little left hand support for same. The quick guide provides limited documentation and the Bloody website for software download. While I did not spend a lot of time with the software, it allows macros and some customization—although no changes to lighting for the B720.

If there is anything someone might not like about the Bloody B720 it would probably be the font selection for the keys (a bit sci-fi) along with an inability to control the lighting a bit more fully. For me, it is fine, I like the way it behaves during use and in its more animated modes.

Bloody Gaming may or may not be right about their own proprietary keys becoming popular, since gamers have definite preferences and a multitude of experiences with a variety of keyboards. I can say, that for the type of work and play I do on a daily basis, I would recommend this keyboard to anyone thinking about a mechanical keyboard. The Bloody B720 is an attractive solid keyboard providing a great mechanical key typing and gaming experience.

With that said, it's a good quality tool.  I would buy it again for something with smaller keys.  Just don't bother for the mechanical das keyboard.

reviews.title: Not doublshot
reviews.text:
These two keys: < and > have already worn through. These are not double shot keycaps. They are a semi-translucent keycap with a finish over it that wears through. Doubleshot has an injection molded insert inside the keycap with the translucent area cut out of the keycap.
Should you not care about that, here is some more info. They look okay in the light, but in the dark there is a strong screen glare off of them that can be distracting.

I rate one star for lying about the nature of the product.

reviews.title: Arrived missing key caps but overall pretty good if you have the right keyboard
reviews.text:
Not a keyboard, just key caps to "bling out" your existing keyboard.

I purchased the following keyboard and these key caps fit perfectly. The keyboard (not this item, which is only key caps) comes with a red plastic tool that you can use to pop out the key caps:

Redragon K551 Mechanical Gaming Keyboard RGB LED Rainbow Backlit Wired Keyboard with Red Switches for Windows Gaming PC (104 Keys, Black)

Even the font on the key caps matches the keyboard I bought separately, and the backlighting shines through the font.

The only down side: the set of key caps is missing about 1/4 of the keys (see attached picture).

I would give it all five stars if it had arrived containing all the key caps advertised in the product listing. It was a surprise prank gift for a coworker, which is why I made do with what we got.

reviews.title: ULTRA Smooth, ZERO scratch and SAVES your RGBs, what else do you want?
reviews.text: These are so smooth you wont even believe it until you put it up to your ear and will hear nothing going on on the inside of the switch. After trying the Oil Kings and being super impressed, I said fine lets try the CJ Dark Blue Lubed switch and see what the fuss is about and I am glad I did. It is the end game switch. Stop the search right here. These are dead smooth a 10 out of 10, OK are about 7-10 and the Gateron Red Pro are somewhere like 5-10. They are all hard to tell and distinguish between the smoothness but once you test them out to your ear/finger you will be able to tell. I don't believe you need to lube these because well, they come lubed and since they are so smooth I don't see a need. Also the benefit with this switch is that this CJ Dark Blue comes with a slot for the LED to shine through just fine, so you will save your RGB as I have and I am glad.

title: CUSTOMIZATIONS Coiled USB c Cable, Customized Gaming Mechanical Keyboard Cable, Handwork Braided + PET mesh, Metal Plug, with Detachable Metal Aviator Connector (Black White)
features: [Multiple color options] CUSTOMIZATIONS coiled keyboard cable has various eye-catching color settings, which can provide more interesting elements for your desktop settings.

[keyboard Coiled cables, durable quality]: This custom coiled cable uses four high-quality copper wires and aluminum shielding foil as the core. The material is not afraid of bending or breaking. The weaving adopts woven mesh + PET mesh double-layer weaving, and the resin mesh is both beautiful and durable.

[Metal aviator connector]: The metal cannon connector allows you to quickly connect different cables to the keyboard or other accessories. The pure copper is chrome-plated, the copper needle and the copper core are plated with 24K gold wire, and the shell adopts electroplating technology to achieve a bright texture, highlighting high-end and texture.

[Coiled USB C Cable, USB 2.0 compatible] The cable adopts Type-C interface design, aluminum alloy CNC shell, sandblasted surface, corrosion and oxidation resistance, and recessed design can match most keyboards.

[Excellent quality]: This Coiled Cable has experienced multiple technical tests, including data transmission stability, Cable is firm and durable, and the joint welding is firm. Each product you have got is high quality.

reviews.title: Works great
reviews.text: This USB C cable works great! It's appears to be high quality material and I really like the coiled style. Very impressed!

title: YUNZII Keynovo V2 Coiled Keyboard Cable with Aviator USB Cable for Type-C Mechanical Gaming Keyboard (White)
features: * 【MULTIPLE COLOR CHOICES】Every keyboard needs a cable. The Keynovo V2 coiled keyboard cable features various striking color setups. It's got all the features you need, and it comes easy to match your different keyboard setups.

【4 PINS AVIATOR & EASY INSTALLATION 】the coiled keyboard cable features a detachable 4 pins metal aviator port to support a wide range of connections. The keyboard cable takes USB-A side with metal aviation, Type-C plug, two-section cable connection, and detachable design. The coiled keyboard cable delivers a high-performance in every aspect, elegant addition to any setup.

【 HIGH-PERFORMANCE QUALITY】This performance-tested keyboard cable with a flexible coiled design, metal aviator, durable TPE material. It delivers superior and high-end quality and visual aesthetics. The cable is an ideal replacement cable for any mechanical keyboard.

【LONGER LENGTH AND WIDER COMPATIBILITY】The V2 custom coiled aviator cable has upgraded length wit 155cm straight cable, and 14cm coiled cable. The total length of the cable reaches 170cm. The coil cable diameter is 2cm. Compared to last version, the upgraded V2 keyboard cable can be compatible with most mechanical keyboard, e.g KC84.

* ONLY CABLE INCLUDED!【USB-C INTERFACE, USB 2.0/3.0 COMPATIBLE】Designed to work flawlessly with any USB type C device, the USB to Type-C coiled aviator cable works with mechanical gaming keyboard and type-c mobile phones charging.

reviews.title: Decent. Kind of small coil.
reviews.text:
YUNZII AKKO Coiled Keyboard Cable with Aviator USB Cable for Type-C Mechanical Gaming Keyboard (Sakura)

This cable just ok. It's not bad but definitely lacks the quality feel of others. The coil is small around 1/2" outer diameter. The connections are good and fit in my keyboard well and the aviator cable looks good. For me, the cable from the coil to the USBc connector is a bit too long and looks kind of weird being so far away from the keyboard. Also, the coil just looks like an old phone coiled cable. It just isn't for me, but the price is good, and it doesn't look half bad.

【PBT keycaps】These green keycaps are made of thick PBT material, and the surface of the keycaps has an oil-repellent coating, which can provide you with a good typing experience.

【Wide Compatibility】This set of matcha keycaps has a total of 124 keys, suitable for 61/87/100/104/108 cherry mx switches mechanical keyboard. If you are not sure whether it is suitable for your keyboard, please contact customer service.

reviews.title: Nice keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my Anne Pro case without issue. Nice colors and easy to type with.

reviews.title: Great for the price!
reviews.text: This is a clone set as far as I'm aware, but I don't mind that. The arrows are cuter than the "original" Matcha set that can be found for the same price. These feel great, sound great, look great. I couldn't be happier!

reviews.title: these are sweeeet
reviews.text: They're really good quality for the price! Very retro. Use them to replace the ugly keychron ones you get with a keyboard.

reviews.title: Lovely green color
reviews.text: My keyboard only has like 64 keys so most of them I don’t use. But they are great, easy to put in. Fits my desk theme very well :))

title: SteelSeries Apex Pro Mini Mechanical Gaming Keyboard – World’s Fastest Keyboard – Adjustable Actuation – Compact 60% Form Factor – RGB – PBT Keycaps – USB-C
description: The Apex Pro Mini Wireless brings the world's fastest keystrokes with OmniPoint 2.0, total adjustability of activation points, dual actuation, and a slim-form factor.
features: World's Fastest Keyboard - Overtake standard mechanical keyboards as OmniPoint 2.0 delivers 11x faster response and 10x swifter actuation.
Customize Every Keystroke - Precisely adjust the registration depth of every key to the nearest 0.1MM, going from speedy 0.2MM touches to deliberate 3.8MM presses.
2-in-1 Action Keys - Program two different actions for the same key, such as walking with a light touch and sprinting with a deeper press.
60% Form Factor - Streamlined design saves desk space and leaves more room for mouse movement, retaining full-size keyboard capabilities with side-printed functions.

Durable PBT Keycaps - Long-lasting double shot PBT keycaps provide a high-quality texture for enhanced keystroke feel and improved typing accuracy. Compatibility-OS-Windows, Xbox, PlayStation, and Mac OS X. USB port required(*Not all software features supported on Mac OS). Software-SteelSeries Engine for Windows (8.1 or newer) and Mac OSX (10.13 or newer) (*Not all software features supported on Mac OS)

reviews.title: Almost perfect, if not for the competition
reviews.text:
TLDR: If you don't want to deal with pre-ordering the Wooting60HE or just really like Steelseries products, this is a really good 60% option.

Pros:
-Omnipoint switches feel very good
-Adjustable actuation point
-Finally adopted PBT keycaps for longevity
-Good RGB brightness and accuracy
-Pretty good stabs out of the box made better with some lube

Cons:
-The Wooting60HE exists...

Thoughts:
Rant time. Right out of the gate, this would be the perfect 60% keyboard currently, if the Wooting60HE didn't exist. It has hotswap switches, acoustic foam, analog functionality, adjustable actuation point, RAPID TRIGGER (huge for high actuation point users), pre-lubed switches and stabs, and doesn't require its software to be active to function fully. This board isn't bad by any means, it's just that the wooting is just better in my opinion. I saw steelseries' video today comparing their "response time" with some weird rigged comparison test where they claim a 10ms advantage over the wooting. Even if that were true, which I don't think is accurate, 10ms isn't enough to justify choosing this over the wooting, unless you just really like Steelseries products, which is fine. But the rapid trigger functionality alone is HUGE for switches with a high actuation. If you don't know what that is, basically at any point during the upward travel (after you've pressed a key), you can press down again, to register another keypress. It basically makes the actuation dynamic letting you keep that high actuation, but not require you to fully release the key to press it again. Anyway, rant/PSA over, back to the Apex.

Despite everything I just said, this is still a really good keyboard. If you liked the Apex Pro/TKL, you'll like this. I've found that magnetic switches are easily the smoothest ones on the market, even without lubing. They just glide, absolutely fantastic.

I would comment on the responsiveness, but honestly, I really can't imagine milliseconds making a huge difference for movement inputs in a shooter. Osu probably if that's your thing, but starting to move in one direction or another a milisecond faster isn't going to change your game in the slightest. A mouse, absolutely, but there's really no keyboard input that needs that level of precision. So try not to get hung up on responsiveness in regards to keyboard claims.

Something definitely worth noting is how magnetic switches and optical switches are basically immune to double presses that eventually happen to regular mechanical switches. You can rest assure that all of your key presses will be very accurate.

Conclusion:
Before you commit to this, I highly recommend looking into the Wooting60HE. If you don't want to deal with pre-ordering, or just really like Steelseries products, this is still a really good keyboard. I love the switches, and am so happy they decided to finally start using PBT keycaps.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: I don’t know why people are hating on this keyboard so much? unless you’re like a nerd and actually care about the .2mm and stuff about the price. The keys so really nice and smooth and is a very quiet keyboard, if you want to drop this much for a keyboard then go for it i think it was a good purchase for me

reviews.title: Perfect for Gaming
reviews.text: 15 year old gamer approved! It allows for quicker response times in games, lights are bright and stay on. The keys are a little noisy but if you’re into ASMR, it’s relaxing!

reviews.title: Takes some getting used to.
reviews.text: This mini keyboard was a major change from my previous keyboard. The first major shock I had when I needed the grave/tilde key for programming, thankfully with the SteelSeries GG program I was able to program the grave/tilde key into the escape key with a modifier. In Minecraft as well some of the F3 functions needed to be mapped because there is no function row. After some getting used to I am just as fast on this keyboard as my previous one. There was a quirk I could not figure out without some googling however, if you hold the SteelSeries logo and the windows button it locks the windows button and changes it's color to white. Doing it again will re-enable the windows button.

title: MELETRIX WS Grey Tactile Mechanical Keyboard Lubed Switches(35pcs)
description: WS Grey Tactile Mechanical Keyboard Lubed Switches
features: Nylon Top and bottom housing, POM silent tactile stem
Double stage spring: 63.5g
Total travel: 3.50±0.3mm Pre-travel: 2.00±0.5mm Bottom-out force: 63.5
Factory lubed

reviews.title: Can be used stock!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:528307786b36a4c2c9833ddd389101cb]] They come with a small amount of lube pre applied. Usually, prelubed switches are inconsistent in my experience but these ones are really good. I decided to not lube them at all. EDIT: got a few more batches, they're not as consistent. But seller was nice enough to get them replaced.

The stems are long pole. As a result, it has a nice bottom out sound.

It was noticeably heavier coming from a 50g switch to these that are 63g, but overtime I got adjusted and had no problems typing with them.

Overall a great buy, though I wish it was priced more competitively.

Using on Drop Preonic with MT3 Camillo, plateless, tape mod

reviews.title: Nice linears
reviews.text:
Starting off not sure who's at fault for this as it's only fulfilled by Amazon but one of my bags was missing a few switches (one bag was open and all the switches were rattling around inside the amazon pouch it arrived in) I counted 5 missing and there wasn't a silica gel packet either, the other bags were fine.
complaint aside they feel really nice and the factory lube seems to be well done as wish they had a "3 pin" option and different switch amounts 35 2 is too few and 3 is too many (unless some get lost in transit like mine lol) 90 would be a good option for the TKL/75% gang

reviews.title: Will be Using Right Away
reviews.text: In building & repairing computers these key switches are used very often. Mainly for high-end (over $100) keyboards that the serious gamers use. What they do is tough on the keyboards and often switches break or become less responsive which may lead to a gamer losing a match. These snap right in to the sockets and can instantly rebuild the whole keyboard like new. I realize they would not be used by your everyday person or casual gamer but when it gets serious they are  the switches to be used.

reviews.title: Nice Linear
reviews.text: These are nice and smooth and sound wonderful.  I'm not a linear fan usually, but I do like them in my modifier keys (shift, backspace, enter, and space) because I don't like the return sound a tactile makes while in a larger key.  These are perfect for that as they don't have a high pitch and there's no spring ping to even think of!  I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lighter-weight linear switch with some style.

reviews.title: Great Stock!
reviews.text: The switches sounds great and feels good to use. The lube in this is not bad at all and you don't hear any ping or scratchiness. The bottom out is much more than I am used to, but I do enjoy it otherwise.

title: YUNZII AKKO CS Switch, 3 Pins for DIY MX Mechanical Keyboard, 45 pcs (Ocean Blue Switch)
features: 【About AKKO CS Switches】 AKKO Custom Series (CS) switches are the newly designed 3-pin switches for customers who have specific typing and gaming needs. Each package has 45 switches. Six types of switches are available and no matter which kind of typing feeling you want, we cover it all.

【Compatibility】AKKO CS switches are designed with a general MX structure so that they can fit most keycaps sets. The switch has 3 pins but also fit in all 3 pins and 5 pins keyboards. Based on the strong compatibility, you can easily change the switch and customize your mechanical keyboard.
【Built-in LED Slot 】The AKKO CS switches are equipped with a north-facing LED slot for LED modification. It is also compatible with SMD LED, provides more fun and options for keyboard enthusiasts.

【Durable Lifespan & Improved Performance】With a lifespan of approximately 50 million times of keystrokes, you are assured of high quality and a long lifespan. The CS switches feature a spiral spring design that can make the typing more comfortable.

【Ocean Blue Tactile Switch】The tactile bump of the Ocean Blue switch is at 0.55mm and it ends at 1.88mm, which gives long-continued feedback. Different from the blue and brown switch, the Ocean blue switch has a medium tactile position that can give you an excellent experience in typing and gaming.

reviews.title: Very good for the price
reviews.text: I'm not a mechanical keyboard connoisseur, but needed to replace the switches on my cheap Redragon keyboard to get rid of the loud clicking noise. The original switches were blue switch clones and the clicks will wake up the neighbors. These are cheap enough that I decided to give them a try. They are pretty good in terms of touch and sound, from my perspective and my use. Will definitely resort to these again in the future if I need to replace keyboard switches, especially for budget keyboards.

reviews.title: Clean key caps
reviews.text: Using these key caps on a Corsair K70 pro mini wireless and they look great. They feel good and match my purple and white theme

title: KINESIS USB-A Freestyle Pro Quiet Ergonomic Split Mechanical Keyboard (Cherry MX Silent Red Switches)
features: AWARD-WINNING SPLIT DESIGN: Advanced ergonomics for all body types and preferences via adjustable split, splay, and tent (tenting accessories sold separately). New cable storage compartment supports up to 20" of separation when you need it.

GENUINE CHERRY MECHANICAL SWITCHES FOR PREMIUM TYPING FEEL AND PERFORMANCE WITH LESS NOISE: Cherry MX "Silent" red mechanical switches feature a low activation force, linear feel and are rated for 50M key presses for unmatched durability.

FULL ONBOARD PROGRAMMABILITY WITH THE DRIVERLESS SMARTSET ENGINE: 9 custom layouts, 8 office hotkeys, on-the-fly macro recording, one-touch key remapping, pre-configured Mac and Dvorak layouts, settings saved to keyboards 4MB flash memory, and the SmartSet App GUI for Windows & Mac.
PLUG-AND-PLAY WITH ALL MAJOR OPERATING SYSTEMS: Windows 7-10, Mac OS X, Linux, Chrome.
2 YEAR MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY: Buy with confidence knowing that Kinesis engineers all our products in the USA to the highest standards and stands behind them 100 percent since 1992.

reviews.title: Good, but could be better
reviews.text:
I use this with my MacBook Pro. I have brutal pain in my hands, wrists and forearms from osteoarthritis, tendon-patchy and tendonitis; caused from 19 years of being at my computer and holding my phone up for blogging.

This keyboard has made things so much better, however... I needed the keyboard to have the lift without the wrist pads which are agony for my hands. Only, the tent system is made to include all pieces; the lift and the wrist pads. So, I have to use the lift pieces without the wrist pads which leaves a chunk of awkward plastic sticking straight out from the keyboard; something I am constantly catching. It's frustrating to no end.

I am now looking for something I can simply glue to the inner, underside of the keyboard that will add the lift but leave the front open.

I'm also disappointed with the price. Come on now. It's sooooo overpriced. It's only a matter of time before another brand comes out with a more reasonable one- but in the meantime, I feel like they are taking advantage.

In the end, this is far better than nothing. It definitely places your hands in a more ergonomic position, and is better than a flat keyboard or laptop touchpad setup.

reviews.title: Great keyboard for ergonomics
reviews.text: The split keyboard layout has been great for my shoulders and arms.  It took a couple of weeks for me to get used to the layout but the extra functions offered on the edges of the keyboard are very helpful.  Being able to replace the key caps is huge.  I really love this keyboard.

reviews.title: Quality material, but as wide as TKL keyboards
reviews.text:
I wish the length of the cable was longer, and that a longer cable can be purchased separately.
For the price, the tenting should be standard, but it is a separate purchase. And, without them, this keyboard isn't as great.

I purchased the tenting separately, and it really helps with ergonomics when typing. But, when gaming it tends to push my hands side and down, so it's better to use a separate palm support.

I was surprised that when the keyboard is laid out, it is as wide as a ten keyless keyboard. I was hoping that it would be shorter, so that the mouse has more room. It's fine for work or just typing. For gaming, I'll put the right section farther up. Or, I'll put it above the keyboard tray, so I still have access to the keys when needed. That frees up a lot of room for the mouse.

The quality of the keyboard is great. Everything feels solid. The keys feel wonderful. And, the ergonomics is great for typing.
The tech support was friendly and helpful. I had a bit of a problem early on with customizing the keys, and their tech support promptly helped me.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: Nice quiet keyboard with a great feel.  I got the tenting and wrist rest kit.  It took a little bit to get used to the angle but I'm really enjoying it and have already programmed some of the keys to do routine things for me.

reviews.title: Perfect balance of ergonomics and key comfort.
reviews.text:
I have tried all the Kinesis keyboards with the exception of the 400$ console setup. I have chosen this one as the best overall experience, specifically paired with the VIP3Pro tent and wrist accessory. The keys on this keyboard have a much more consistent and pleasant feeling as compared to their membrane offerings as well as their silent switch option. Those keyboards are fantastic if you are not already addicted to the feeling of mechanical keys. However if you are searching for the ultimate in keyboard experiences for daily use, I heavily and without reservation recommend this keyboard set. The keyboard may initially seem expensive when compared to other ergonomic keyboards that do not split or offer the tenting/wrist rests, however having tried many different curved and non split ergonomic options from Microsoft, Logitech, and many others, I can say without a doubt that the optimal configuration requires the keyboard to be split. If you need to have a number pad like I do, I suggest any simple number key option, they'll fit nicely between the two halves and do not interfere at all. I plan to purchase a second keyboard for my WFH setup to compliment my work setup. I don't want to go back to a standard keyboard and you won't either after using it.

This review was not endorsed, sponsored, or influenced by any 3rd party, I am simply that overjoyed with the product.

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

reviews.title: Looks like durgod k320
reviews.text: Awesome caps.  Looks exactly lie durgod k320

reviews.title: Very sturdy product. Was as described
reviews.text: I bought it as a gift for my grandson. Just what he wanted. Working good

reviews.title: Good Keycap Set! Beat Expectations
reviews.text:
I had initially bought this keycap set as a placeholder for my GMK Samurai Keycap set that I was set to receive. However, I had learned that Drop would ship them in late July, and since I was unsure if I was getting a refund, I bought this keycap set as a placeholder.

This keycap set broke all expectations that I held for cheap keycaps. They feel to be of good quality, with a high-ish thock sound that is what I wanted. Overall, it is a good keycap set that I would recommend to people if they are looking for good quality and good-sounding keycaps.

Also, I did get a refund for the GMK keycaps so I will be sticking with these keycaps, although they don't match as they were not meant to pair together and were rather a last-minute decision.

title: IDOBAO 10pcs MA Profile Keycaps 1u Ball Shape for Mechanical Keyboard DIY Blank Keycaps (Blue)

reviews.title: Good quality
reviews.text: Good quality and even better sound. Slightly pricey for the quantity but very much worth it.

reviews.title: Very nice coiled keyboard cable.
reviews.text:
The cable came nicely coiled with no flaws. Plugs are high quality and the screw connection type is pretty heavy duty I don't see this breaking. Only wished the straight part of the cable was longer.

I highly recommend this cable for anyone looking for a custom looking cable that wont break the bank.

reviews.title: Works as advertised!
reviews.text: This cable worked exactly as advertised. My keyboard has a USB c connector, and it fit together perfectly. I love the look of this cable as it neatly tucks under my desk and doesn't leave any unnecessary excess hanging around. Overall very happy with this product!

reviews.title: Decent aviator cable for my keyboard setup.
reviews.text: These cables are an aesthetically great match for my “rebel scum” keyboard build.  The connections are tight and the materials used seems like they are great quality.  I don’t really have any complaints about the quality of this product.  I think it’s important to note these are not braided cables, and have a soft rubber feel, much like you would get on any standard USB type cable.

reviews.title: Good Quality!
reviews.text: Bright white coiled cable makes my desk look neater and organized. Matches my desk decor better. Keyboard response is the same as the original cable it came with, no complaints here.

reviews.title: Treat your keyboard to a nicer cable
reviews.text: What a nice cable, with solid metal USB plugs, a clean aviator connector, and tight and even coils. I got it in purple, to match some keycaps I like, and it looks perfect. The USB-C plug is about 5.5mm thick, so should fit most recessed plugs. I was able to charge every device I tried, even a chromebook (lenovo duet) and phone (pixel 6a).

title: SteelSeries PRISMCAPS - Double Shot Pudding-Style Keycaps - Durable PBT Thermoplastic - Compatible with a Wide Range of Mechanical Keyboards - White
description: Built for extreme durability with thermoplastic PBT keycaps and with enhanced RGB pudding-style lighting, PrismCaps include 2 additional sets of bottom row keycaps, making them compatible with the widest range of MX stem mechanical keyboards.
features: Ultra-wide compatibility with both standard and non-standard bottom row keycap sets to fit most mechanical keyboard brands, including SteelSeries, Razer, Corsair, and virtually all other MX stem mechanical keyboards
Standard US ANSI 104 keys works on full sized, tenkeyless, and 60% mini keyboard layouts
Enhanced lighting displays brilliant pudding-style RGB illumination through a specially engineered double shot design with translucent sides and letters
Retains consistent texture and appearance even after heavy use with highly durable double shot PBT keycaps
Included keycap puller is built with finger hole grip to pull any keycap out with ease and flexible enough to fit around any standard keycap.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

reviews.title: CUTE!
reviews.text: Love it so much and so cute in person!

title: YMDK 144 Chess Board Keycaps Dye-Sub Thick PBT Cherry Profile Key caps for TKL 61 64 68 75 87 96 104 108 GMMK MX Mechanical Keyboard
description: Package Included 1x Full set Dye sub Cherry Profile Keycap set + Keycap puller No keyboard Included Details This link is only the keycaps,no keyboard included.The keycap loading board is only to take photo,not included in the sold package.We will pack the keycaps in our own carton. The printed ones are dye-sublimation. The keycap is made of durable PBT material,the thickness is about 1.5mm. Special Design,can use both with north south facing PCB Our store has opened DIY services.If you have interests,don't hesitate to leave us message. Compatiability Main 96 layout whose bottom is 1.25 1.25 1.25 6.25 1.5 1.5 1 1 or 1.25 1.25 1.25 6.25 1 1 1 1 1 or 1.25 1.25 1.25 6.25 1.25 1.25 1.25,such as Melody 96 YMD96 Kira 96 RS96 Standard 104 87 61 ANSI ISO layout whose bottom is 1x6.25u+7x1.25u. Compatible with Filco Ducky Poker 61 87 104 which is most common Main 84 layout, compatible with KBD75 YMD75 Keycool 84 XD84 NCR80 (6.25u space) Compatible with Ducky one 2 Mini SF It's compatible with popular 68 layout as well Incompatiability The spacebar is not 6.25u,it can't be compatible.It's not compatible with Cosair Logitech Razer Keyboard. It's not compatible some old Cherry brand keyboard whose caps lock back hole is off center,it can't be compatible,such as 1800 Some stabilizers are little issue with these big keycap, but it is easily fixable. Please contact us if you encounter any similar problem. Please inquire us if you need special keycaps. After Service If some keys missing or broken on the way,just leave us message,we will send you missing or broken ones soon.Don't worry about it & don't leave feedback immediately.
features: Package Included: 1x Full PBT Dye Sub keycaps + Keycap Puller
This keyset is made of PBT.The thickness is around 1.5mm.Special Design,can use both with north south facing PCB
The legend is dye-sublimation which never fades.The injection point is at the corner on the back.
Compatible with most mx switches 61 64 68 75 84 96 104 108 mechanical keyboard whose space is 6.25u.
Compatible with YMD96,Melody96,Kira96,GK64,Tada68,KBD75 KBD67 GMMK etc.Compatible with Cherry 3800 3000 3850.Compatible with SF Mini GK61 Anne Pro

reviews.title: High Quality Caps - Adore the Theme
reviews.text: As a typist, these caps have a great feel to them. They are thick without being heavy. No wobble. Improve the sound of the board and switch. I purchased the Smoke Cloud theme, and the images are adorable for a weather enthusiast like me. This set also had plenty of extra keys to complete the 96% keyboards, which not a lot of sets include. I was leary of paying $40 for a set without trying them first, but I don't regret it - great purchase - fast shipping!

reviews.title: Looks great, sounds like quality
reviews.text: I'm a bit of a keycap junkie.  I love being able to swap keys out to give my keyboard a different look.  If you're looking at this, you likely already like the look and I think they look better in person.  The keycaps are easy to install and provide good sound.  No hollow cheapness to these!

reviews.title: Very thick keycaps
reviews.text:
What I like
I really like the quality of the prints on these keycaps.
Also the thickness of these keycaps are even past some doubleshot ones I have.

What I find questionable.
The character keys looked like they were a light green color, but they are bright white. I ended up liking them anyway, just was a bit surprised.

Overall they are a great set of keycaps, going to keep using them for sure.
